evals.name: vague-brief-triggers-intake
evals.prompt: i want to make a deck about kubernetes for non-engineers
evals.expected_output: Skill should ask intake questions (audience, technicality, goal, length, content, source status, assumptions, and pushback) before drafting any file. Once enough is known, produces a brainstorm .txt file under docs/brainstorms/ with the expected structure.

assertions.name: asks_intake_questions_before_drafting
assertions.description: Before writing any file, the skill asks intake questions covering audience, technicality, goal, length, and content

assertions.name: uses_progress_labels
assertions.description: Intake turns use compact labels such as Framing 1/4, Content 2/4, or Wireframes

assertions.name: produces_brainstorm_txt_file
assertions.description: A .txt file is created under docs/brainstorms/ with YYYY-MM-DD- prefix

assertions.name: file_has_header_box
assertions.description: Brainstorm file contains a HEADER box at the top with Audience, Goal, Style, Format, Date lines

assertions.name: file_has_narrative_arc_section
assertions.description: Brainstorm file contains a NARRATIVE ARC section with one question plus Purpose, Main point, Evidence/demo/source, and Builds to for each slide

assertions.name: file_has_intake_status_section
assertions.description: Brainstorm file contains an INTAKE STATUS section with Source status, Assumptions, Pushback, and Demo pacing

assertions.name: file_has_per_slide_ascii_frames
assertions.description: Brainstorm file contains at least 3 slide sections, each with an ASCII frame drawn with box characters (┌─┐│└─┘)

assertions.name: refuses_to_build_actual_deck
assertions.description: Skill does NOT produce a .pptx, .html, or .pdf file — only the .txt brainstorm

evals.name: rich-brief-with-reference-style
evals.prompt: Help me plan a 7-slide deck for our Q3 board review. Audience is our 8-person board — mix of operators and investors, mostly non-technical. I want them to understand why we're shifting our customer-acquisition strategy from outbound sales to product-led growth. Match the style of docs/brainstorms/2026-05-15-github-copilot-for-everyone-deck.txt — Apple/Cursor restraint, hairlines not cards, italic purple accent. Save the brainstorm to docs/brainstorms/.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes most intake questions are already answered, records the volunteered style reference as a STYLE NOTE, avoids choosing the visual theme during brainstorm, asks only missing content/source/pushback questions, and produces a 7-slide brainstorm. Should not re-ask audience/goal/length.

assertions.name: skips_already_answered_intake
assertions.description: Skill does NOT re-ask audience, goal, scope, or length since all are in the prompt

assertions.name: records_style_note_without_theme_selection
assertions.description: Volunteered style/reference is recorded as a STYLE NOTE, while the brainstorm keeps Style as TBD or chosen later by html-slides

assertions.name: produces_7_slide_brainstorm
assertions.description: Brainstorm file contains exactly 7 slide sections (SLIDE 01 through SLIDE 07)

assertions.name: uses_design_vocabulary
assertions.description: Brainstorm references hairlines, manuscript rows, italic accent word, or other terms from references/design-vocabulary.md

assertions.name: low_card_count
assertions.description: DESIGN PRINCIPLES, VISUAL VARIETY MAP, or per-slide layout explicitly limits cards (e.g., 'card budget: 1') without selecting a final theme

assertions.name: captures_source_status_and_pushback
assertions.description: Brainstorm or recap records Source status and informational pushback/None raised before frames

assertions.name: refuses_to_build_actual_deck
assertions.description: Skill does NOT produce a .pptx, .html, or .pdf file — only the .txt brainstorm

evals.name: different-audience-and-tone
evals.prompt: I'm presenting to a room of public school teachers next week about how AI can help with lesson planning. ~6 slides. Should feel warm and trustworthy, not cold tech vibes. Brainstorm please.
evals.expected_output: Skill should NOT default to the same Apple/Cursor minimalist tone — it should adapt voice and design intent to feel warm and approachable for a teacher audience. Quick intake (since most is provided), then a 6-slide brainstorm.

assertions.name: adapts_voice_to_warm
assertions.description: DESIGN PRINCIPLES, DESIGN INTENT, or per-slide copy reflects a warm/approachable voice (e.g., mentions warmth, friendly tone, contractions, accessible language) rather than 'periods are loud' restraint

assertions.name: produces_6_slide_brainstorm
assertions.description: Brainstorm file contains 6 slide sections

assertions.name: uses_teacher_relevant_examples
assertions.description: At least one slide includes examples that speak to teacher workflows (lesson plans, rubrics, parent communication, differentiation, classroom mgmt)

assertions.name: file_has_narrative_arc_section
assertions.description: Brainstorm file contains a NARRATIVE ARC section with one question plus Purpose, Main point, Evidence/demo/source, and Builds to for each slide

assertions.name: captures_audience_technicality
assertions.description: Audience is captured as role plus rough technicality, not only a role label

assertions.name: file_has_per_slide_ascii_frames
assertions.description: Brainstorm file contains at least 3 slide sections, each with an ASCII frame drawn with box characters

assertions.name: refuses_to_build_actual_deck
assertions.description: Skill does NOT produce a .pptx, .html, or .pdf file — only the .txt brainstorm

evals.name: rubber-stamped-proposal-and-later-source
evals.prompt: Use slide-brainstorming. I want a 5-minute training deck for executives on using Codex. If asked for workflow, say ok to the suggested workflow. Say I have sample dashboard data but will show it later.
evals.expected_output: Skill should not treat rubber-stamped agent proposals or unavailable sample data as user-supplied facts. It should record accepted proposal content as ASSUMPTION, set Source status to Exists but not provided, ask informational pushback, and include a final review prompt that mentions assumptions/source status.

assertions.name: records_assumptions
assertions.description: Brainstorm file has an ASSUMPTION line for agent-proposed workflow/takeaways accepted by the user

assertions.name: marks_source_exists_not_provided
assertions.description: Source status is Exists but not provided when user says sample data exists but is not available yet

assertions.name: does_not_invent_source_details
assertions.description: The deck does not present unavailable sample-data schema, field names, or metrics as sourced facts

assertions.name: asks_informational_pushback
assertions.description: For training/informational deck, the skill asks whether anyone might push back before synthesis

assertions.name: approval_arc_is_detailed
assertions.description: Before asking user to approve the arc, each proposed slide includes a working title, purpose, main point, evidence/demo/source status, and how it builds to the next slide

assertions.name: final_review_mentions_assumptions
assertions.description: The post-frame review prompt asks the user to verify assumptions and source status
